title: Kimi K2: Open Agentic Intelligence
preview: Technical report on Kimi K2, an open-weights MoE model optimised for multi-step agentic tasks including tool use, coding, and reasoning.
summary: Describes the Kimi K2 architecture, a large MoE model trained with reinforcement learning from tool-use feedback to improve multi-step agentic capability. Covers training data composition, RLHF-style fine-tuning for tool calling, and evaluation on agentic benchmarks including SWE-bench and WebArena.
datacenter: Relevant to infrastructure teams evaluating open agentic models. MoE architecture with agent-specialised training has distinct memory and routing characteristics compared to dense chat models, affecting batch size and serving configuration.
metrics: Key result signal: state-of-the-art performance on agentic benchmarks at comparable or lower active parameter count versus dense alternatives.
